title: Buttery Gooey Korean Smoked Turkey Neck Stir-fry
description: A weeknight KOSHER-friendly dish featuring slow-cooked and smoked turkey necks in a savory, buttery sauce with vegetables for a comforting and indulgent meal.

ingredients.name: Vegetables (e.g. Carrots, Bell Peppers, Zucchini)
ingredients.amount: 1 med each, sliced into thin sticks

instructions: Season turkey necks with salt and pepper, then place them in a single layer on a baking sheet. Roast for about 45 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While turkey necks are roasting, heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onion and garlic to the heated oil and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until softened.
Remove turkey necks from the oven and slice them into bite-sized pieces. Return turkey necks to the pan with vegetables, then add Korean BBQ Sauce, Soy Sauce, Fish Sauce, Gochujang, and Water.
Stir everything together well, cover, and cook for about 15 minutes on low heat.
Add sliced vegetables to the skillet and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with steamed rice or noodles.
